edges.description: Reduced H3K9me2 at the GRIN1 promoter leads to upregulation of NMDA receptor subunit 1 and aberrant neuronal network activity.
EHMT1 deficiency derepresses miRNAs that target REST/NRSF, reducing REST protein and prematurely activating neuronal gene programs.
Disrupted neuronal network dynamics and synaptic plasticity underlie cognitive impairment and language deficits.
Imbalanced excitatory neurotransmission through NMDAR hyperfunction contributes to epileptogenesis.

nodes.description: EHMT1 (also known as GLP) encodes a SET domain-containing histone methyltransferase that forms a heterodimeric complex with EHMT2 (G9a). This complex catalyzes mono- and dimethylation of histone H3 at lysine 9 (H3K9me1/me2), an epigenetic mark associated with transcriptional silencing at euchromatic loci. Haploinsufficiency of EHMT1 leads to reduced H3K9me2 levels, resulting in aberrant gene expression across multiple tissues, particularly in the developing brain. The minimal critical region for the syndrome has been narrowed to less than 1 Mb comprising EHMT1.

evidence.snippet: haploinsufficiency for EHMT1 is causative for 9q subtelomeric deletion syndrome
these changes are mediated by upregulation of NMDA receptor (NMDAR) subunit 1 correlating with reduced deposition of the repressive H3K9me2 mark, the catalytic product of EHMT1, at the GRIN1 promoter

nodes.description: In EHMT1-deficient human cortical neurons, reduced H3K9me2 at the GRIN1 promoter leads to upregulation of NMDA receptor subunit 1 (NMDAR1/GluN1), driving aberrant neuronal network activity with reduced burst rate, longer burst duration, and increased temporal irregularity. Pharmacological inhibition of NMDARs rescues the network phenotype, establishing a direct mechanistic link between EHMT1 deficiency and NMDAR hyperfunction that provides a potential basis for targeted therapeutic approaches.

evidence.snippet: Neuronal networks of patient-derived cells exhibit network bursting with a reduced rate, longer duration, and increased temporal irregularity compared to control networks
we rescue the KS patient-derived neuronal network phenotypes by pharmacological inhibition of NMDARs

nodes.description: EHMT1 deficiency leads to derepression of multiple miRNAs that target the neuronal transcriptional repressor REST/NRSF. EHMT1 normally maintains H3K9me2 marks at miRNA transcription start sites, keeping them silenced. When EHMT1 is haploinsufficient, these miRNAs are upregulated, reducing REST protein levels and prematurely de-repressing neuronal gene programs. This disrupts the timing of neurodevelopment and contributes to the aberrant neuronal differentiation seen in Kleefstra syndrome.

evidence.snippet: reduced EHMT1 activity decreases NRSF/REST protein leading to abnormal neuronal gene expression and progression of neurodevelopment in human iPSC
EHMT1 regulates NRSF/REST indirectly via repression of miRNA and leads to aberrant neuronal gene regulation and neurodevelopment timing

nodes.description: Epilepsy occurs in a minority of patients. Seizure types are variable and onset is typically in childhood.
